title: OXO Good Grips 2-Cup Angled Measuring Cup
description:
About this item
Patented angled surface lets you read measurements from above
Eliminates the need to fill, check and adjust
Soft, non slip handle
Standard and metric measurement markings
BPA free
Dishwasher safe
reviews_5s:
Don't believe some of the negative reviews. This measuring cup is perfect for almost every liquid and the convenience of being able to read it looking down from the top is primo. I'm going to address some of the bogus negative comments I've read:

It measures differently looking down from the top compared to looking at the markings on the side. Not true. Looking at the side, you use the bottom of the meniscus that forms with water-based liquids to measure -- not the top of the meniscus. Looking from the top, you can't see the meniscus, so the top of the liquid actually touches the line, When you compare the two, if you're reading the bottom of the meniscus looking at the side of the cup and you have the top of the liquid just touching the mark and not going over, you'll find the two methods are totally spot on, one with the other.

It doesn't pour well. It pours perfectly for me, every time. I frequently use it to pour water into my electric drip coffeemaker and also to boil just the right amount in my teakettle to brew a personal-sized pot of tea (I don't want to boil more water than I need because we're experiencing a horrible drought and water is a precious resource). Nothing dribbles down the outside of the cup, and the stream of liquid being poured goes right where I want it to go.

These measuring cups can't nest. Well, I suppose if you had two of the same size, say two of the 2-cup size I'm reviewing here, it wouldn't nest. But I can tell you that I also own the 1-cup size and the small 1/4 cup (2 fl.oz.) size and they nest perfectly, readily and easily. That's how I store them, in fact. I'm considering ordering the 4-cup size, but I seldom have need for one that large, so I haven't bought one yet.

These are very hard to clean if you measure oil in them. Perhaps if you are doing it by hand, but I put mine in the top rack of the dishwasher and that cleans it perfectly. (To prevent etching of glassware, I fill the uncovered detergent cup with liquid automatic dishwasher gel detergent, and I put a dishwasher detergent tablet in the covered cup. Works like a charm.)

I still have my set of Pyrex measuring cups and if I'm boiling water in the microwave oven or melting butter or chocolate, I use my Pyrex cups for that. I also use the Pyrex cups to measure honey or syrup, because I want to get a sticky liquid out of the cup completely using a silicone spatula. For such a liquid, in these OXO cups, scraping a sticky liquid out with a spatula is difficult because of the "stepped" oval measuring ring provided for top viewing. That said, I don't measure syrup or honey all that often.

I suppose if I were trying to use metric measurements all the time, I wouldn't like it so much. The ring shows cups on one side and fluid ounces on the other. Metric markings are only on the side. But I seldom have to measure anything using the metric system, since U.S. recipes provide liquid quantities in cups or fluid ounces.

I simply wouldn't want to be without one of these wonderful measuring cups that you can read without crouching down to look at the side.This OXO BRAND liquid measuring cup is accurate and easy to read. Comfortable to use and great value.This is the best measuring cup for ease of use while pouring. It’s hard to tell when measurements are on the side of other containers if you put too much or too little with the inside measuring lip this makes pouring fool proof. The sturdiness of material is great. This is my second cup only because my husband lost the first one in the garage.Perfect two cup measuring. Good handle grip. I just received it and used it for measuring my King Arthur Buttermilk pancake mix which calls for two cups. I made the best pancakes, high and fluffy. I’m sold!Have you ever tried to measure liquid for a recipe and had to bend down to see if you got it right? Well, I found this measuring cup from OXO Good Grips, and it's been a game changer in my kitchen! It holds up to 2 cups and has this awesome feature where you can see how much liquid you have from above. No more squatting down to check the side of the cup!

I really love how easy it is to use. Now, when I'm cooking or baking, I can quickly check my measurements and keep going. It makes things so much faster and less of a hassle.

If you're like me and want to make your time in the kitchen a little easier, this measuring cup is a great tool to have. It's simple, but it makes a big difference. Plus, it's from OXO, so you know it's good quality and will last a long time.Does what it is supposed to, super easy to read! I recommend. It is nice not to have to crouch down to read where the level is, just simply look down! I recommend and the price is great! I've had this for a few months. It washes great!So easy to read the amount of liquid without having to hold it up to see. Highly recommendThis was purchased to replace an older version of this same measuring cup. In addition to being new of course, this updated version seems to be improved for reading from above. The handle design is also nicer and the pouring spout has been updated. We only replaced since the old one broke (after ~15 years).Good quality, easy and clear to read without bending down to read on sides or lifting up might be crooked. OXO known for quality and fair price. I prefer glass as for durability but it's heavy I store on top of shelf no need to worry about falling on my head or shattered.Bought for a gift and couldn't find one in the stores! Have one myself - love the idea of not tilting my head one way or another to get the cup reading. A bit expensive but it was a gift ....
reviews_1s:
Great design and feel but incorrect Measurement markings, it's off by 1/8 of a cup, tested it with 2 different Pyrexs and a measuring cup, as long as you test yours and account for the difference, not worth returning it... Oxo needs to do better...The design of these measuring cups is completely flawed. The handles are hollow and allow water to enter the handle if submerged in the sink or dishwasher. This winds up with old water sitting inside the handles until the next time you use the cups, and I've dumped old stagnant water into a recipe a few times.

I took the handles off mine and am now using the Oxo "good grips" without the grips, but when these wear out I'll be switching brands.Maybe these wouldn't have so many glowing responses if people actually double checked the accuracy of this measuring cup.

Major issue:
-the inside measurements from 1.25 to 2.0 cups dont agree with those on the outside of the cup. The scale on the inside of the cup reads about 1/8th of a cup more than what is actually there.

I checked them with steel liquid measuring cups and water and it turns out that all of the outside measurements
are correct from 0-2 cups. The 2 scales on the inside, both cups and oz., are wrong from 1.25 cups through 2 cups. They are accurate however from 0 to 1 cup.

If I want to have to look at the outside of the measuring cup to see what I have, I'll just use a Pyrex cup. The inside measurements, which are the whole reason for buying these, are useless if they are not accurate. I don't want measurements that are "about right". These are worthless to me. Going back.Picture shows cup has measurements listed in cups and ounces but it came with mL.the 2oz mark is not at all accurate. We were checking our 2oz bottles and noticed, based on this measuring cup our industry standard bottles are too big and we were over filling them to make them look full. I decided to bring my good ole Pyrex measuring cup into work with me and found the 2oz mark to be WAY off on the OXO version. My Pyrex filled to 2oz filled my 2oz bottle perfectly. Very dissatisfied as the whole point of this cup is to measure properly.I was shocked when this thing would not pour correctly. At first, I thought it was me when liquid leaked all over the place when I poured. But no. It leaked all over the place no matter what I did or what angle I tried - over and over again. This kind of bad engineering I would expect from a cheap measuring cup. But not this brand. I could not believe the Oxo can't handle this basic function. I bought another brand. DO NOT BUYPut it in the dishwasher a single time, on the top rack, and it came out all cloudy and splotchy. Nothing will fix it. Still totally usable, but looks super ugly now, like I took sandpaper to half of it.

I own a ton of OXO stuff and have never had a problem like this before. Normal Whirpool dishwasher, normal 7th Generation dishwasher detergent. Stick to glass Pyrex instead I guess.I'm normally a fan of OXO products and own many of their gadgets. These measuring cups are the first problem product I've had from OXO...both the 2 cup and 4 cup sizes cracked on the bottom of the cup, right at the plastic injection point (that little circle with the tiny bump in the center of it), and they both now leak at the crack. These cracks developed very soon after I bought them, and I only ever washed them in on the top rack of the dishwasher. Because of the design of the cups, it's kinda annoying to wash them by hand - and they're not great for measuring dry ingredients for the same reason. So mine have gone into the garbage can, replaced by the KitchenAid cups of similar appearance (although without the handy interior measuring marks). My advice, keep your receipt if you buy these. Or skip them and get the KitchenAid, which have a non-slip rubber ring on the bottom, another nice feature. And they won't crack in the dishwasher.I am a huge fan of OXO products for kitchen and baby products this is the 3rd measuring cup(took a lot to break the last cup) I have ordered and when washing both cups you can definitely feel a difference in the strength of the plastic the 2nd cup bent more easily and felt lighter. I can tell it won’t last long, it feels like dollar tree material tbh.It says dishwasher safe but came out of the dishwasher looking like this
